summary: Base64 + Buffer decode pattern in ts-eslint-helper@4.0.2 lacks corroborating context
details: index.js contains a Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString('utf8') decode pattern at line 8 (and a second Buffer.from at line 107). This pattern is a common building block for both legitimate uses (decoding embedded data, parsing JWTs, handling binary assets) and obfuscated payload reconstruction. No further evidence of credential access, environment scraping, outbound network exfiltration, lifecycle hooks, or fetch-and-execute behavior was observed in the package contents. The package name resembles a TypeScript/ESLint helper utility, but the limited file count (4) and the presence of base64 decoding warrant a human reviewer to confirm whether the decoded content is benign data or a hidden code payload before clearing it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
